scope.target: Normalize test-spec storage to remove redundant duplicate domain/aggregate folder names
constraints: Default structure should stay easy to scan for current one-domain-one-aggregate cases
Retain an escape hatch for deeper aggregate subfolders when a domain eventually has multiple aggregates
Explain the storage rule explicitly in guide files
non_goals: Do not redesign production spec layout
Do not populate all missing test specs in this normalization task

inputs.architecture_target: Test-spec structure should be semantically clear without introducing redundant folder depth.
review_findings: Current test-spec tree contains repeated folder names such as order/order and inventory-item/inventory-item.
The extra aggregate layer does not add information when a domain currently maps to a single primary aggregate.
user_constraints: Settle the structure first before expanding more Stage 4 content.

steps: Flatten current test-spec examples to .dev/specs/tests/<domain>/ by default.
Update guides to explain when an aggregate subfolder is optional rather than mandatory.
Preserve the option to reintroduce aggregate subfolders only when a domain owns multiple aggregates.
validation: Current test-spec examples no longer use duplicated folder names.
Guides explain the default and optional deeper layout clearly.
The structure remains extensible without being noisy.
deferred_items: Additional test-spec examples for the rest of the active use cases.

results.summary: Flattened the current test-spec examples and clarified that aggregate subfolders are optional and only needed when a bounded context has multiple aggregates.
